run_summary: Screen 1 faces a multi-trigger sympathy basket (Meta Pocket, Google Gemini variants, Anthropic Sonnet 5) with 6 new OVERDONE flags, all meeting BUY eligibility. Portfolio already holds 4 of those tickers (AEIS, PLAB, DOCN, EVH) at 8, 7, 5, 1 days respectively; two are underwater but thesis-intact, two are profitable. New flags on AEIS/PLAB/DOCN/EVH confirm thesis integrity. Two fresh names (ONTO, DUOL) clear the bar. The basket rule applies: 6 names from related AI-event triggers triggers exploratory tier for all, except ONTO (materially cleaner filings + none threat + higher conf) earns a single conviction slot.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
entries.thesis: A Meta consumer gaming app has zero mechanical connection to semiconductor process-control equipment demand. If anything, AI chip manufacturing requires MORE inspection/metrology, not less. The sharp drop reflects semi-cap sympathy selling on general tech weakness, not any substantive Pocket-related threat. Curation false-victim thesis is confirmed.
entries.what_kills: A major fab capex cut, ONTO customer concentration loss, or the pending Rigaku deal falling apart with negative implications.
entries.reasoning: ONTO is the sole name in the 6-flag basket with materially sharper filings evidence + 'none' threat_assessment (not 'minimal'). 10-K explicitly frames ONTO as process-control/metrology supplier for advanced semiconductor nodes—direct AI-chip-fab beneficiary. Meta Pocket (consumer gaming) has zero mechanical linkage to fab capex or process control. Filings are concrete and specific ('process control/metrology supplier for advanced semiconductor nodes'). Confidence 4, threat 'none', panic 'unjustified' all confirm. Basket rule permits one conviction promotion when filings evidence is materially sharper. Size conviction tier (20% × confidence 4 = 20% of equity).

red_team.weakest_link: The move is already down 12.46% in one day and 10.65% over five days, creating the risk that the 'sympathy fade' correction is already complete and the bull case is pricing in the full rebound.
red_team.critique: ONTO has already experienced a sharp one-day decline of 12.46% and a five-day decline of 10.65%, suggesting the sympathy selloff is already largely executed. The bull thesis assumes Haiku can buy into a temporary panic, but if institutional sellers have already cleared positions and the stock is stabilizing here, the fade is already played. The filings evidence is strong (process-control linkage to fab capex is real), but that evidence existed before the drop—what matters now is whether the catalyst (Meta Pocket app) has already wrung all the fear out.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Gemini Spark, Google's agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
entries.thesis: Gemini Spark is a consumer/prosumer Mac agent — it has zero business-model overlap with DOCN's SMB/developer cloud infrastructure. If anything, more agentic AI usage drives more inference compute demand, which DOCN monetizes. The sharp drop looks like generic 'AI news = bad for smaller cloud' reflex selling, unsupported by the filings.
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler pricing action targeting SMB developers, or DOCN customer concentration in productivity-SaaS end-markets that themselves get hit by agents (unlikely per the customer base description).
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Meets BUY eligibility (OVERDONE, conf 4, threat 'none', panic 'unjustified', not already held, 10-K frames AI/ML as growth vector for developer inference/compute). Basket rule applies (Gemini Spark catalyst is part of 6-flag set). DOCN filings are solid (AI/ML growth narrative) but threat is 'none' only via absence of negative text—not as concrete as ONTO's explicit fab-capex linkage. Default exploratory. Size exploratory (6% of equity).

red_team.weakest_link: The filings evidence that threat is 'none' rests on absence of negative text in the 10-K rather than explicit concrete linkage between AI inference workloads and DOCN's SMB developer monetization model, making the threat assessment weaker than ONTO's explicit fab-capex connection.
red_team.critique: DOCN's threat assessment of 'none' is inferred from the absence of negative text in filings rather than affirmative evidence that Gemini Spark drives inference compute demand for SMB developers on DOCN's platform. The five-day move of 2.7% is the smallest in the basket, suggesting the market has already largely ignored this sympathy trigger. If DOCN's SMB customer base does not translate agent inference into incremental GPU/compute spend (a behavioral assumption not tested in the filings), the bull thesis collapses. The exploratory tier and 6% sizing reflect this subordinate confidence level appropriately.

entries.name: Evolent Health, Inc
entries.catalyst: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
entries.thesis: Evolent's product is regulated payer contracts plus specialty clinical networks plus risk-sharing capital — a cheaper Anthropic agent model does not displace a Medicaid managed-care contract or an oncology risk corridor. The down move on an Anthropic agentic-model day is thematic SaaS-sympathy selling against a name whose real risks (Medicaid redeterminations, oncology cost trend, OBBBA policy changes) have nothing to do with LLM pricing.
entries.what_kills: A subsequent customer-loss 8-K or downward guide tied to a specific payer renegotiation — those would reveal the move was idiosyncratic, not sympathy.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Meets BUY eligibility (OVERDONE, conf 3, threat 'minimal' not 'none', panic 'unjustified', not already held). Basket rule applies (Anthropic Sonnet 5 catalyst is part of 6-flag set). Threat is 'minimal' (not 'none'), and filings show only generic AI risk mention, not concrete evidence that payer contracts/managed-care model is untouched. Exploratory tier appropriate. Size exploratory (6% of equity).

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day change shows EVH UP 12.92% while the stock is classified as OVERDONE and rated a BUY, creating ambiguity about whether this is a sympathy-fade play or a false-positive signal (the stock may already be rebounding for unrelated reasons).
red_team.critique: EVH is the only ticker in the basket showing a positive five-day return (up 12.92%) while classified as OVERDONE and given a BUY signal. This contradicts the sympathy-fade thesis: if EVH has already bounced, then the entry point is less clear and the move may reflect idiosyncratic healthcare or payer-specific news rather than Anthropic Sonnet 5 sympathy. The threat assessment is 'minimal' not 'none,' and the filings show only generic AI risk mention per Haiku's own reasoning. The exploratory tier and 6% sizing are appropriate given the weaker threat rating and the confusing price action, but the fact that EVH is already up while peers are down suggests Haiku may be chasing a rebound that is already in price.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
entries.thesis: A consumer AI gaming app from Meta has no bearing on data-center power-conditioning demand. AEIS's own filings describe AI-driven data center capex as a growth tailwind, not a threat. The sharp drop appears to be broader semi-cap/data-center capex-peak fear rather than any Pocket-related linkage. Curation false-victim hypothesis is confirmed by filing text.
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler capex cut announcement, a major AEIS customer concentration loss, or evidence that AI data center power designs are moving to competitors.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The five-day decline of 17.03% is materially steeper than peers (ONTO at 10.65%, PLAB at 12.15%), suggesting AEIS-specific weakness unrelated to Meta Pocket sympathy that the bull case has not acknowledged. Bear case: AEIS has fallen 17.03% in five days versus 10.65% for ONTO and 12.15% for PLAB, a spread too wide to attribute to sympathy selling alone. This suggests either AEIS-specific customer/margin concerns in recent filings, or a steeper correlation with hyperscaler capex slowdown signals that the bull thesis glosses over by citing 'AI data-center capex as growth tailwind.' The volume multiple of 0.97 (below average) also suggests sellers are not pushing hard, which could indicate the stock has already repriced to reflect real near-term weakness rather than irrational panic. The exploratory tier and 6% sizing acknowledge lower conviction, but the asymmetric five-day move relative to peers is a named mechanical weakness. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: Meets BUY eligibility (OVERDONE, conf 4, threat 'none', panic 'unjustified', not already held, 10-K confirms AI data-center capex tailwind). However, AEIS is part of the 6-flag Meta Pocket / Gemini / Anthropic basket—basket rule applies. AEIS filings are strong but not uniquely sharper than ONTO; both are data-center/chip-fab adjacencies. Default to exploratory tier per basket rule. Size exploratory (6% of equity).)

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day decline of 17.03% is materially steeper than peers (ONTO at 10.65%, PLAB at 12.15%), suggesting AEIS-specific weakness unrelated to Meta Pocket sympathy that the bull case has not acknowledged.
red_team.critique: AEIS has fallen 17.03% in five days versus 10.65% for ONTO and 12.15% for PLAB, a spread too wide to attribute to sympathy selling alone. This suggests either AEIS-specific customer/margin concerns in recent filings, or a steeper correlation with hyperscaler capex slowdown signals that the bull thesis glosses over by citing 'AI data-center capex as growth tailwind.' The volume multiple of 0.97 (below average) also suggests sellers are not pushing hard, which could indicate the stock has already repriced to reflect real near-term weakness rather than irrational panic. The exploratory tier and 6% sizing acknowledge lower conviction, but the asymmetric five-day move relative to peers is a named mechanical weakness.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
entries.thesis: Photomasks are required for every chip manufactured, including AI accelerators. A Meta consumer AI gaming app is orthogonal to this supply-chain step. The modest sympathy drop appears purely thematic. Curation false-victim thesis holds.
entries.what_kills: A slowdown in chip design activity, loss of a major foundry customer, or export-control escalation targeting PLAB's China operations.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry guard: PLAB was closed 8d ago for a gain (realized 8.79%). Re-opening a recently-closed name requires flag confidence >= 4; this flag is confidence 3. Downgraded to WATCH. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: Meets BUY eligibility (OVERDONE, conf 3, threat 'none', panic 'unjustified', not already held, filings confirm photomask demand tied to chip design not consumer AI). Basket rule applies (6-flag trigger set). Filings are clear but not materially sharper than ONTO (which has chip-fab adjacency AND 'none' threat AND conf 4). Default exploratory. Size exploratory (6% of equity).)

red_team.weakest_link: The confidence level is only 3 (versus ONTO's 4) and the five-day move of 12.15% is significant, raising the question of whether the sympathy drop has already corrected enough to make exploratory sizing the only prudent call.
red_team.critique: PLAB's five-day decline of 12.15% is material and the confidence is lower (3) than ONTO (4), which correctly places this in exploratory tier. The photomask thesis is mechanically sound (masks are required for all chips), but the lower confidence and already-executed drop reduce the margin of safety. The move_pct of 4.78% decline and five-day 12.15% suggest the initial panic is priced in; if the sympathy rebound does occur, PLAB's lower conviction rating means Haiku has less edge than with ONTO.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Gemini's personalized AI image generation now free for US users
entries.thesis: Today's image-gen announcement is functionally irrelevant to Duolingo's product (text/audio-driven habit loop with gamification). Retail reflexively sells edtech on any frontier-AI release, but Gemini personalized image generation is not the LLM-tutor threat that would actually erode DUOL's TAM. The moat is the dopamine/streak loop, not content uniqueness, and DUOL is also already extended down on the week — sympathy compounding rather than fundamentals.
entries.what_kills: Google announces a Gemini-powered language tutor directly competing with DUOL; DAU growth visibly decelerates in the next quarterly print; Duolingo Max subscriber metrics disappoint.
entries.reasoning: Meets BUY eligibility on classification (OVERDONE), confidence (3), and panic_calibration ('unjustified'). However, threat_assessment is 'indirect' not 'minimal' or 'none'. Filings acknowledge GenAI as competitive risk; Gemini image generation is not a direct tutoring threat, but the filing language shows DUOL does flag AI as a competitive vector. Basket rule would apply (Gemini image-gen catalyst), but threshold question is whether 'indirect' threat clears the bar. Conservative call: WATCH this one—let the market confirm that Gemini image-gen produces no tutoring disruption before committing capital. If DUOL holds up or recovers slightly over next 2-3 days, can flip to BUY exploratory.
